First, we express all the terms as powers of 2.
$8 = 2^3$, so $8^6 = (2^3)^6 = 2^{18}$.
$16 = 2^4$, so $16^{-3} = (2^4)^{-3} = 2^{-12}$.
Then the expression becomes
$$ (2^{18} \cdot 2^{-12}) \div 2^{-12} = 2^{18-12} \div 2^{-12} = 2^6 \div 2^{-12} = \frac{2^6}{2^{-12}} = 2^{6 - (-12)} = 2^{6+12} = 2^{18} $$
Now we calculate $2^{18}$.
$2^{10} = 1024$.
$2^{18} = 2^{10} \cdot 2^8 = 1024 \cdot 256$.
$1024 \cdot 256 = 1024 \cdot (200 + 50 + 6) = 1024 \cdot 200 + 1024 \cdot 50 + 1024 \cdot 6$
$1024 \cdot 200 = 204800$
$1024 \cdot 50 = 51200$
$1024 \cdot 6 = 6144$
$204800 + 51200 + 6144 = 256000 + 6144 = 262144$
Alternatively, we can calculate $2^{18}$ as follows:
$2^1 = 2$
$2^2 = 4$
$2^3 = 8$
$2^4 = 16$
$2^5 = 32$
$2^6 = 64$
$2^7 = 128$
$2^8 = 256$
$2^9 = 512$
$2^{10} = 1024$
$2^{11} = 2048$
$2^{12} = 4096$
$2^{13} = 8192$
$2^{14} = 16384$
$2^{15} = 32768$
$2^{16} = 65536$
$2^{17} = 131072$
$2^{18} = 262144$

Thus, $(8^6 \cdot 16^{-3}) \div 2^{-12} = 2^{18} = 262144$.
